name: SingleGraph

members.name: SingleGraph
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Creates an empty graph.
 *
 * @param id
 *            Unique identifier of the graph.
 * @param strictChecking
 *            If true any non-fatal error throws an exception.
 * @param autoCreate
 *            If true (and strict checking is false), nodes are
 *            automatically created when referenced when creating a edge,
 *            even if not yet inserted in the graph.
 * @param initialNodeCapacity
 *            Initial capacity of the node storage data structures. Use this
 *            if you know the approximate maximum number of nodes of the
 *            graph. The graph can grow beyond this limit, but storage
 *            reallocation is expensive operation.
 * @param initialEdgeCapacity
 *            Initial capacity of the edge storage data structures. Use this
 *            if you know the approximate maximum number of edges of the
 *            graph. The graph can grow beyond this limit, but storage
 *            reallocation is expensive operation.
 */

tags.name: id
tags.description: id must be a non-empty string

tags.name: strictChecking
tags.content: If true any non-fatal error throws an exception.
tags.description: strictChecking must be a boolean value

tags.name: autoCreate
tags.content:
If true (and strict checking is false), nodes are
           automatically created when referenced when creating a edge,
           even if not yet inserted in the graph.
tags.description: autoCreate must be a boolean value

tags.name: initialNodeCapacity
tags.content:
Initial capacity of the node storage data structures. Use this
           if you know the approximate maximum number of nodes of the
           graph. The graph can grow beyond this limit, but storage
           reallocation is expensive operation.
tags.description: initialNodeCapacity must be a non-negative integer

tags.name: initialEdgeCapacity
tags.content:
Initial capacity of the edge storage data structures. Use this
           if you know the approximate maximum number of edges of the
           graph. The graph can grow beyond this limit, but storage
           reallocation is expensive operation.
tags.description: initialEdgeCapacity must be a non-negative integer

members.name: SingleGraph
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Creates an empty graph with default edge and node capacity.
 *
 * @param id
 *            Unique identifier of the graph.
 * @param strictChecking
 *            If true any non-fatal error throws an exception.
 * @param autoCreate
 *            If true (and strict checking is false), nodes are
 *            automatically created when referenced when creating a edge,
 *            even if not yet inserted in the graph.
 */

tags.name: strictChecking
tags.content: If true any non-fatal error throws an exception.

tags.name: autoCreate
tags.content:
If true (and strict checking is false), nodes are
           automatically created when referenced when creating a edge,
           even if not yet inserted in the graph.

members.code:
/**
 * Creates an empty graph with default edge and node capacity.
 *
 * @param id
 *            Unique identifier of the graph.
 * @param strictChecking
 *            If true any non-fatal error throws an exception.
 * @param autoCreate
 *            If true (and strict checking is false), nodes are
 *            automatically created when referenced when creating a edge,
 *            even if not yet inserted in the graph.
 */
public SingleGraph(String id, boolean strictChecking, boolean autoCreate) {
    this(id, strictChecking, autoCreate, DEFAULT_NODE_CAPACITY, DEFAULT_EDGE_CAPACITY);
}
